title: How to selectively log full email threads without "Auto-Log" enabled?

posts.author: _Aya (Aya)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone, I’m looking for a way to selectively log emails to Monday.com without compromising privacy.

The Scenario:
Our team does not want to enable the “Log all outgoing/incoming emails” feature because many of our emails are private. To maintain control, I’ve opted to use the BCC to Monday address method for specific items.

The Problem:
When I respond to an existing email thread and BCC the Monday address, only my specific response is logged to the timeline. I lose the entire preceding conversation (the “thread history”), making the log entry contextless and difficult for the rest of the team to follow.

The Goal:
I want to selectively log an email and include the previous thread history, without having to log every single email that passes through my inbox.

Questions:

Is there a way to “force” the BCC method to capture the thread history?

How are other privacy-conscious teams handling this?

Thanks in advance for any tips or workarounds!

posts.author: drtanvisachar (Dr. Tanvi Sachar- Certified Monday.com Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @_Aya !

The BCC method only logs the specific email that’s sent to the monday address. It doesn’t reconstruct the full email thread, so the previous conversation usually won’t appear in the item’s updates.

There isn’t a way to force the BCC feature to pull in the earlier thread history. It simply records the content of the email that was forwarded or copied.

A few teams handle this by forwarding the full email instead of replying with BCC. Most email clients include the previous messages when you forward a thread, so the logged update contains the full context.

Another option is to paste a short summary or the key part of the thread into an item update when the conversation becomes relevant to the team. It adds a small manual step, but it keeps control over what gets logged while avoiding private emails being captured automatically.

If preserving thread context is important, it may also be worth raising the use case with monday’s support team, since logging partial threads is a common limitation with the selective BCC approach.
